trends.description: Global public optimism recovering — 71% expect 2025 will be better than 2024, up from 70% last year — even as structural concerns about prices, technology, and security remain elevated.

trends.name: AI Expected to Lead Decision-Making
trends.description: Global public predicting AI will begin to meaningfully influence major decisions in business, government, and personal life in 2024 — marking a shift from AI as tool to AI as decision agent.

trends.name: AI Arms Race Investment Acceleration
trends.description: Technology companies accelerating AI infrastructure investment at unprecedented pace — creating an AI-driven capital expenditure cycle that is one of the defining investment themes of 2025.

sources.report: J P MORGAN - 2025 Year-Ahead Investment Outlook_CAIG

trends.name: Post-Cyclical Normalization in US Economy
trends.description: US economy normalizing across growth, jobs, inflation, and earnings after the extraordinary post-pandemic cycle — entering a more conventional macro environment with different investment implications.

sources.report: J P MORGAN - 2025 Year-Ahead Investment Outlook_CAIG

trends.name: New Economic Era — Higher Growth and Returns
trends.description: Global economy entering a new era characterized by stronger capital investment, higher interest rates, and better growth prospects than the low-growth, low-rate decade that preceded it.

trends.name: AI Boom or Bust Decision Point
trends.description: 2025 identified as a critical year for AI investment thesis validation — with capital expenditure acceleration requiring evidence of returns that markets are beginning to demand.

trends.name: Healthcare Disruption by AI and Automation
trends.description: Healthcare sector facing AI-driven disruption as automation, robotics, and AI diagnostics begin reshaping care delivery economics and the role of human practitioners.

trends.name: Power Infrastructure as Strategic Investment Theme
trends.description: Energy grid and power infrastructure emerging as a primary investment theme driven by AI data center demand, EV adoption, and industrial reshoring — creating decade-long capital deployment opportunity.

trends.name: Brand Collaboration as Retail Differentiation
trends.description: UK retail embracing unexpected brand collaborations and new designer partnerships as primary mechanisms for creating excitement and distinctiveness in a competitive physical retail environment.

trends.name: Hostile World Seeking Safety
trends.description: Global consumers experiencing the world as genuinely hostile — with uncertainty, anxiety, and geopolitical tension driving a primary desire for security, safety, and predictable refuge.

trends.name: Micro Happiness as Coping Strategy
trends.description: In the absence of macro stability, consumers cultivating small, personal moments of genuine happiness — micro-scale joy becoming a primary psychological survival mechanism and consumption driver.

trends.name: Testosterone Fury — Masculinity in Crisis and Reaction
trends.description: Male identity crisis generating both extreme reactions (toxic masculinity amplification) and genuine recalibration — with 'testosterone fury' shaping politics, media, and brand strategy in 2025.

trends.name: Realverse — Authenticity Over Metaverse
trends.description: Consumer appetite for authentic, tangible reality growing in reaction to digital saturation — prioritizing real experiences, real community, and real emotion over virtual and synthetic alternatives.

trends.name: Polycrisis as Cultural Operating System 2024
trends.description: The convergence of geopolitical, economic, technological, and social crises creating a permanent polycrisis state — not a temporary disruption but the new structural backdrop for all human activity.

trends.name: Collapsing Truth and Information Decay
trends.description: Accelerating collapse of shared truth — enabled by social media, AI, and political polarization — making consensus reality structurally unstable and communication fundamentally adversarial.

trends.name: All Are Creators
trends.description: Universal democratization of content creation — with every individual now having the tools, platform, and audience potential to function as a media entity — reshaping attention, influence, and cultural production.

trends.name: Controversial AI — 'Polemic Intelligence'
trends.description: AI becoming a source of genuine societal controversy rather than merely a technology — with its impacts on jobs, truth, creativity, and power generating organized resistance and political mobilization.

trends.name: Innovation Supercycle Defining the Decade
trends.description: A multi-decade innovation supercycle — driven by AI, biotech, energy transition, and computing — creating the dominant structural growth force for the 2020s and reshaping capital allocation globally.

trends.name: Out-of-System Assets Rising
trends.description: Assets outside traditional financial system — gold, Bitcoin, real assets — gaining allocation as investors hedge against fiscal risk, currency debasement, and institutional fragility.

trends.name: China's Balance Sheet Recession
trends.description: China entering a balance sheet recession — with deflation, property sector distress, and deleveraging combining to create a Japan-1990s-style drag that reorients global growth and investment flows.

trends.name: Intergenerational Wealth Transfer as Global Priority
trends.description: Record intergenerational wealth transfer underway as Baby Boomer wealth passes to younger generations — creating new demands for wealth management, estate planning, and financial education at scale.

trends.name: Great Normalization After Pandemic Extremes
trends.description: Global economy undergoing normalization from extraordinary pandemic-era distortions — with interest rates, growth, and inflation converging toward sustainable secular levels that define a new equilibrium.

trends.name: Multipolarity and Strategic Reshoring
trends.description: Global economy restructuring around a multipolar world order — with strategic reshoring, friend-shoring, and active industrial policies relocating supply chains and manufacturing capacity.

trends.name: Media Industry Identity Crisis
trends.description: Media ecosystem experiencing a crisis of confidence and identity — with convergence of market volatility, evolving audience behaviors, and AI creating fundamental uncertainty about media's future purpose and structure.

sources.report: KANTAR MEDIA - 2025 Media Trends and Predictions Report_CAIG

trends.name: Audience Measurement Redefinition
trends.description: Traditional audience measurement paradigms breaking down in a fragmented media world — with new standards needed for cross-platform, AI-influenced media consumption that current tools cannot capture.

sources.report: KANTAR MEDIA - 2025 Media Trends and Predictions Report_CAIG

trends.name: Connected Innovation Integrating Launch and Advertising
trends.description: Brand growth requiring the integration of innovation, advertising, and brand experience as a connected system — with brands that align all three growing at double the rate of those relying on any single element.

trends.name: Brand Control Eroding in Creator Economy
trends.description: Brand control over messaging weakening as influencer-created content and social media amplification create risks and opportunities that traditional brand management frameworks cannot fully govern.

trends.name: AI Measurement and Attention Prediction Scaling
trends.description: AI-based creative measurement and attention prediction tools scaling rapidly in marketing — enabling pre-testing and optimization that reduce waste and improve campaign effectiveness.

trends.name: Generational Spending Divergence Under Inflation
trends.description: Inflation affecting all consumers but creating opposite behavioral responses by generation — with younger consumers more optimistic and maintaining discretionary spend while older consumers retrench.

trends.name: India-Bharat Digital Divide as Growth Dynamic
trends.description: India's consumer culture bifurcating between urban digital natives ('India') and the vast domestic market of traditional consumers ('Bharat') — with brands needing to serve both simultaneously.

trends.name: Default Mode Online for Indian Consumers
trends.description: Indian consumers moving into a permanent 'default mode online' state — where digital access, social media, and e-commerce are the default frame for consumption, information, and social life.

trends.name: Compulsive Convenience vs. Conscious Consumption
trends.description: Indian market tension between the pull of frictionless digital convenience and the growing awareness of its environmental and social costs — creating a values-behavior gap that brands must navigate.

trends.name: Healthcare Investment Innovation Driving Deal Market Evolution
trends.description: Healthcare and life sciences investment market adapting to absorb breakthrough innovations — with biopharma, medical devices, and healthcare IT all requiring new deal structures and valuation frameworks.

trends.name: Strong AI as Foresight Horizon
trends.description: KPMG foresight identifying the emergence of strong AI — capable of general reasoning and autonomous action — as the most transformative signal on the horizon, with implications across work, ethics, and governance.

trends.name: Systemic Risk Convergence
trends.description: Climate, AI, geopolitical fragility, and brittle social structures converging to create systemic risks that interact and amplify each other — requiring integrated foresight beyond siloed risk management.

trends.name: Trusted Transformation as Technology Investment Imperative
trends.description: Technology transformation requiring explicit trust frameworks — with organizations that combine innovation ambition with governance rigor outperforming peers on both security and business outcomes.

trends.name: Do More with Less Technology Mandate
trends.description: Technology leaders facing pressure to accelerate digital transformation while operating with smaller budgets — driving efficiency, prioritization, and ROI discipline into previously expansion-oriented tech functions.

trends.name: Fragrance's Male Appeal Driving Beauty Growth
trends.description: Male fragrance market becoming a significant growth driver in beauty — with men's fragrance social media engagement and influencer-driven purchase behavior creating a structural shift in beauty category dynamics.

trends.name: Global Flavor Fusion as Food Innovation Engine
trends.description: Food and beverage innovation accelerating around exotic, cross-cultural flavor combinations — with regional authenticity and bold unconventional pairings becoming primary product differentiation vectors.

trends.name: AI Enhancing Food Flavor Development
trends.description: Artificial intelligence entering the flavor development process — enabling new ingredient combinations, predictive consumer preference modeling, and accelerated R&D cycles in food and beverage.

trends.name: Personalized, Decentralized, and Green Healthcare
trends.description: Healthcare evolving simultaneously toward greater personalization, geographic decentralization from hospitals to homes and communities, and sustainability — three structural shifts converging to transform care delivery.

trends.name: AI Accelerating Drug and Vaccine Development
trends.description: Big data and AI compressing pharmaceutical development timelines — with AI-enabled target identification, trial design, and biomarker analysis creating step-change improvements in R&D productivity.

trends.name: Peak Rates as Investment Inflection
trends.description: Interest rates approaching or at cyclical peak in 2024 — creating an investment inflection that repositions fixed income, reduces refinancing risk, and opens valuation opportunity in rate-sensitive assets.

sources.report: M&G INVESTMENT_Peak views what lies ahead in 2024

trends.name: Fragmenting Global Village
trends.description: The global village concept fragmenting as national sovereignty, cultural identity, and political polarization push toward localism and tribalism — reversing decades of globalization's homogenizing momentum.

trends.name: Burrowing In — Retreat to the Private Sphere
trends.description: Consumers retreating into private, controllable spaces — homes, close relationships, local communities — as the global environment feels threatening and institutions feel untrustworthy.

trends.name: Death of Hustle Culture
trends.description: Hustle culture exhaustion reaching a generational tipping point — with the relentless productivity ethos of the 2010s giving way to intentional rest, boundaries, and anti-overwork values.

trends.name: Lifecare — Holistic Life Management
trends.description: A new holistic concept of care emerging that integrates health, financial wellbeing, and life management — with consumers seeking integrated support systems rather than fragmented service providers.

trends.name: AI as Creative Muse
trends.description: AI transitioning from productivity tool to creative inspiration source — with artists, writers, and creators using AI to unlock new creative directions rather than merely automate existing work.

trends.name: Slowing Tech's Roll — Technology Backlash
trends.description: Growing pushback against technology's pace and pervasiveness — with consumers, regulators, and policymakers seeking to slow, constrain, and redirect technological change toward human values.

trends.name: AI in B2B Content Strategy
trends.description: B2B marketing organizations adopting AI for content creation, personalization, and distribution — with sustainability and authenticity as key strategic overlays shaping responsible AI deployment.

trends.name: Veil of Influence Lifted — Audience Awareness Increasing
trends.description: Audiences becoming increasingly aware of influencer marketing's commercial mechanics — with rising scrutiny, deinfluencing, and criticism of inauthenticity marking a maturation of the influencer relationship.

trends.name: Year of the Girlie — Female Cultural Ascent
trends.description: 2023 marked by celebrations of female achievement and joy — Barbie, Taylor Swift, Beyoncé, Women's World Cup — signaling a structural moment of cultural investment in and celebration of women.

trends.name: Social Media as Primary Brand Channel
trends.description: Social media completing its transition from supplementary to primary brand communications channel — requiring fundamentally different creative, measurement, and organizational capabilities from brands.

sources.report: MCCANN_Social Media Trends Set to Define 2024

trends.name: Sporting Goods Industry Rebalancing Growth and Productivity
trends.description: Sporting goods sector navigating a new balancing act — with slowing growth forecasts compelling simultaneous focus on revenue growth and productivity improvement after years of demand-driven expansion.

trends.name: Mindful Consumption and Indulgent Snacking Coexisting
trends.description: Consumer snacking behavior simultaneously trending toward mindfulness and guilt-free indulgence — with 'better for you' and 'permissive treat' consumption both growing, driven by different need states.

trends.name: Snacking as Evergreen Consumer Ritual
trends.description: Snacking maintaining its central role in daily life across all demographics, geographies, and economic conditions — proving resilient to health trends and economic pressure as a core consumption behavior.

trends.name: Snackification of Food Culture
trends.description: Meal occasions fragmenting into continuous snacking moments as lifestyles become more fluid — with traditional meal structures giving way to continuous grazing patterns that reshape food manufacturing and retail.

trends.name: Middle Class Expansion Driving Global Snacking Growth
trends.description: Expanding middle classes in emerging markets driving next wave of snacking growth — with rising disposable income creating first-time access to branded snack products for hundreds of millions of consumers.

trends.name: Social Media Intelligence as Business Imperative
trends.description: Social media evolving from communications channel to strategic business intelligence source — with 2025 social teams focused on converting social data into actionable organizational insight.

sources.report: MELTWATER - 2025 State of Social Global Report_CAIG

trends.name: Descent from Peak Rates — Policy Navigation Challenge
trends.description: Central banks navigating the technically complex descent from peak interest rates — with inflation-growth balance delicate enough that miscalibration could trigger either recession or reflation.

trends.name: Crypto Industry Post-Credit-Crisis Rebuild
trends.description: Crypto industry rebuilding from its first credit crisis — with survivors (Coinbase, Kraken, Circle) operating on stronger foundations while Wall Street institutional embrace becomes imminent regardless of skepticism.

trends.name: AI and Crypto Convergence
trends.description: AI and cryptocurrency systems beginning to intersect — with AI agents potentially needing crypto payment rails and blockchain infrastructure providing the trustless settlement layer for machine-to-machine economic activity.

trends.name: Social Media Platform Fragmentation Accelerating
trends.description: Social media landscape spreading across an expanding number of platforms — with Metricool's 8-platform analysis revealing no single platform dominance and increasing audience and content distribution complexity.

trends.name: Ontological Shocks Disrupting Trend Methodology
trends.description: Unexpected discontinuities — conflicts, climate disasters, technological ruptures — exposing the limitations of conventional trend forecasting and demanding scenario-based, uncertainty-embracing foresight approaches.

trends.name: AI Redefining Knowledge Work Fundamentally
trends.description: AI and the future of work entering a rare transformative moment where decades-old work models are challenged — requiring research to actively shape outcomes rather than describe outcomes already determined.

trends.name: Hybrid Work Becoming Permanent Architecture
trends.description: Hybrid work transitioning from emergency accommodation to permanent organizational design — with new norms, tools, and management practices needed for a world where presence and location are variable.

trends.name: Reinvention Over Reset as Consumer Mindset
trends.description: Consumer psychology shifting from reset (returning to normal) to reinvention (building something new) — with community, connection, and revitalization driving consumer behavior into 2025.

trends.name: Technology and Wellness Fusion
trends.description: Technology and wellness converging into integrated lifestyle systems — with consumers adopting tech-enabled wellness practices that make digital and physical health management inseparable.

trends.name: Typography as Cultural Signal Across AI Era
trends.description: Typography evolving as a primary cultural signal in response to AI-generated visual content — with human-designed type asserting identity, authenticity, and values in a world of algorithmically produced imagery.

trends.name: Visual Language Responding to Social Contract Shifts
trends.description: Graphic and typographic design reflecting and responding to changing social contracts around freedom, justice, generational continuity, and environmental responsibility — design as social document.

trends.name: GenAI Operationalization Year
trends.description: 2024 marked as the year of operationalizing generative AI — moving from LLM experimentation to production deployment, data contracts, and reliable AI pipelines integrated into business systems.

trends.name: Data Reliability Engineering as AI Foundation
trends.description: Data reliability engineering emerging as a critical discipline as AI systems require trustworthy data — with data observability and quality infrastructure becoming prerequisites for production AI.

trends.name: Fiscal Forever — Government Spending as Structural Force
trends.description: Government fiscal expansion transitioning from election promise to structural reality across developed markets — with high public spending becoming the permanent operating environment for financial markets.

trends.name: Generative AI Entering Game Development
trends.description: Generative AI beginning to transform game development workflows — from AI-generated 3D worlds to AI-assisted content creation — marking the dawn of a new era in interactive entertainment production.

trends.name: Transmedia Gaming Convergence
trends.description: Gaming properties expanding across film, TV, merchandise, and live experiences — with game IP becoming the dominant entertainment franchise format and transmedia releases reshaping the industry.

trends.name: GLP-1 Satiety Shift Reshaping Consumption
trends.description: GLP-1 drugs (Ozempic, Wegovy) creating a new normal where satiety is decoupled from fulfillment — consumers on appetite-suppressing medications developing new consumption patterns that reshape food, beverage, and retail.

trends.name: Impulse and Craving Suppression as Cultural Shift
trends.description: A broader cultural movement toward deliberate restraint from impulse and craving — extending beyond pharmacological satiety to a values-based muting of overconsumption signals that redefines status and awareness.

trends.name: Post-Irony Fashion — Authentic Messiness
trends.description: Fashion trend toward genuine, unpolished aesthetics — cowboy core, meme style, kitsch — as consumers seek authentic self-expression over aspirational perfection, challenging traditional luxury and status signals.

trends.name: Craftsmanship Renaissance in Fashion
trends.description: Intricate handcraft — jacquard, crochet, embroidery — re-emerging as a premium fashion signal in reaction to mass production and digital uniformity, with made-by-hand becoming a luxury differentiator.

trends.name: Radical Uncertainty as Strategic Condition
trends.description: The pace of change — driven by generative AI above all — making conventional trend forecasting structurally unreliable, demanding organizations prepare for radical uncertainty rather than predicted futures.

trends.name: Measurement Confidence Crisis in Digital Marketing
trends.description: Marketers experiencing a crisis of confidence in their ability to measure holistic marketing ROI — with increasing channel complexity, privacy changes, and fragmented attribution creating structural measurement gaps.

trends.name: CTV Becoming Primary Digital Ad Channel
trends.description: Connected TV receiving disproportionate share of global advertising budget growth — with brands shifting TV budgets toward CTV as streaming viewership scales and targeting capabilities mature.

trends.name: UK Online Landscape Reaching Saturation
trends.description: UK internet penetration and online service usage approaching saturation levels — shifting the competitive dynamic from access growth to attention retention, content quality, and safety regulation.

trends.name: AI Enabling Contextual Advertising Renaissance
trends.description: AI making contextual advertising — targeting based on content environment rather than user identity — newly powerful, offering a privacy-safe targeting alternative as identity signals degrade.

trends.name: Shifting Eyes to Ears for Overstimulated Audiences
trends.description: Audience attention migrating from visual to audio media as visual content saturation drives overstimulation — with podcasts, audio advertising, and voice interfaces gaining share of engagement time.

trends.name: Social 3.0 — Culture-First Brand Reset
trends.description: Social media entering its third era — moving from brand broadcasting to conversation to genuine cultural participation — requiring brands to operate as culture contributors rather than interrupters.

trends.name: Bottom-Up Culture Emergence
trends.description: Cultural production shifting decisively from top-down institutional creation to bottom-up, bubble-up emergence from communities and creators — with brands needing to follow and amplify rather than direct.

trends.name: Return of the Spectacle — Live Events Back
trends.description: In-person events and live spectacles returning with full force in APAC — with audiences starved of shared physical experience driving demand for concerts, sports, and brand events beyond pre-pandemic levels.

trends.name: Regulatory Overreach on Big Tech Accelerating
trends.description: Governments and data regulators across APAC accelerating crackdowns on Big Tech — with data sovereignty, platform liability, and competition regulation creating a fundamentally more constrained operating environment.

trends.name: AI-Generated 3D Worlds Enabling New Entertainment
trends.description: AI systems capable of generating playable 3D worlds (like Google's Genie 2) emerging at the Slush frontier — signaling a transformative shift in how interactive entertainment, simulation, and training environments are created.

sources.report: OMG FUTURES - Closer to the Edge_CAIG

trends.name: Sustainability as Standard in Tech Startups
trends.description: Sustainability moving from differentiator to baseline expectation at the technology startup frontier — with environmental design built into products from inception rather than added as a feature.

trends.name: Real World Asset Tokenization as Web3 Growth Driver
trends.description: Tokenization of real-world assets (RWA) — real estate, commodities, financial instruments — emerging as the primary growth vector for Web3 infrastructure, connecting blockchain systems to traditional asset classes.

trends.name: Rococo and Maximalist Aesthetic Revival
trends.description: Rococo-inspired opulence, ornate detail, and ultra-feminine aesthetics returning across home décor, fashion, and celebrations — driven by Gen Z and Boomers seeking theatrical beauty after years of minimalism.

trends.name: Surrealism Entering Everyday Consumer Aesthetics
trends.description: Surrealist visual sensibility — gravity-defying centerpieces, fantastical elements, dreamlike décor — moving from art world into mainstream consumer celebrations and home design choices.

trends.name: Youth AI Ambivalence — The AI Debacle
trends.description: Young consumers simultaneously fascinated by and anxious about AI — welcoming its capabilities while fearing its implications for jobs, creativity, and truth, creating a generation-defining tension with technology.

trends.name: Youth Nostalgia as Coping Mechanism
trends.description: Young consumers turning to nostalgia when times are tough — with economic anxiety, global instability, and uncertain futures driving demand for familiar cultural touchstones from recent decades.

trends.name: Sustainability Becoming Non-Negotiable Business Requirement
trends.description: Sustainability transitioning from strategic differentiator to baseline business requirement — with climate-driven disruption, regulatory enforcement (CSRD), and investor demands making sustainability integration mandatory.

trends.name: Great Sustainability Bifurcation
trends.description: Business world splitting into those embedding genuine sustainability transformation and those making incremental adjustments — with the gap between leaders and laggards becoming a material competitive and regulatory differentiator.

trends.name: AI in Sustainability — Benefits vs. Costs
trends.description: AI simultaneously offering significant sustainability optimization capabilities and creating new environmental burdens (energy, water, e-waste) — with organizations needing to explicitly weigh AI's net sustainability impact.

trends.name: Sustainable Luxury as Standard, Not Premium
trends.description: Luxury brands required to demonstrate genuine sustainability — not as positioning but as operational reality — with climate disruption making sustainability a prerequisite for luxury's promise of lasting value.

trends.name: Transparency as Competitive Advantage in Luxury
trends.description: Trailblazing transparency about supply chain, environmental impact, and social practices becoming a genuine luxury competitive advantage — with consumers and regulators demanding substance over sustainability storytelling.

trends.name: Authentic PR as Primary Communications Paradigm
trends.description: Public relations shifting toward genuine authenticity — real voices, real beliefs, meaningful storytelling — as audiences distinguish performative purpose from actual organizational values and behavior.

trends.name: Employee Advocacy as PR Infrastructure
trends.description: Employee advocacy becoming a primary PR channel — with authentic employee voices generating trust and reach that paid media and corporate messaging cannot replicate in a skeptical information environment.

trends.name: Relentless Relevance as Brand Survival Requirement
trends.description: Brand relevance becoming a survival requirement rather than a differentiator — with 90% of brands raising relevance scores as the penalty for irrelevance grows in a crowded, distracted consumer environment.

trends.name: Emotional Resonance Driving Brand Value
trends.description: Heart-based brand connection — emotional resonance, values alignment, and personal meaning — becoming increasingly important relative to rational product features and functional benefits in driving brand choice.

trends.name: AI Transforming PR Workflows
trends.description: AI becoming the dominant trend shaping PR in 2024 — with 36% of PR professionals citing AI as the top trend, moving from content generation to data analysis, research, and strategic insight generation.

trends.name: AI Integrating into All Commerce Functions
trends.description: AI shifting from tactical tool to integral commerce infrastructure — embedding into content creation, data organization, search queries, product development, and fulfillment in 2024.

trends.name: Retail Media and Commerce Landscape Unification
trends.description: Retail media and commerce technology maturing toward greater standardization — with data measurement, attribution, and platform capabilities converging toward more unified infrastructure in 2024.

trends.name: APAC Real Estate Pivoting to Alternatives
trends.description: Asia-Pacific real estate investors pivoting from traditional office and retail to alternative asset classes — logistics, living, and data centers — as structural shifts in work and commerce reshape demand.

sources.report: PWC_Emerging Trends In Real State APAC 2024

trends.name: Office Market Reset in APAC
trends.description: Office deal volumes plummeting in major APAC markets as repricings lag reality — with foreign funds treating China as too risky and regional investors migrating to quality assets in more stable markets.

sources.report: PWC_Emerging Trends In Real State APAC 2024

trends.name: Cybersecurity at the Epicenter of Innovation
trends.description: Security function repositioning from risk management to innovation enabler — with organizations that embed security into technology development gaining speed-to-market advantage over those that apply it afterward.

trends.name: Cybersecurity Progress Stagnating Despite Budget Growth
trends.description: Security spending and ambition growing while actual security improvement stagnates — with breach costs rising, cloud attack vectors unmanaged, and organizational complexity preventing effective security practice.

trends.name: Crypto Regulatory Frameworks Restoring Trust
trends.description: Global regulatory and legal frameworks for digital assets maturing rapidly — with jurisdictional clarity, EU's MiCA single market, and global standard-setter alignment collectively rebuilding institutional trust in crypto.

trends.name: Tech-to-the-Back — Invisible Technology Branding
trends.description: Leading brands moving technology from front-facing differentiator to invisible infrastructure — letting product experience and human values lead, with AI and tech powering the back end without dominating the narrative.

trends.name: Brand as Advocate for Optimism
trends.description: Brands positioning themselves as genuine advocates for optimism in an anxious world — not toxic positivity, but evidence-based hope that provides consumers a counterweight to pervasive cultural pessimism.

trends.name: AI and Automation Transforming Trading Desks
trends.description: Trading desk workflows fundamentally transforming as AI, big data, and automation compress decision timelines, improve execution quality, and enable best-execution demonstration to regulators.

sources.report: REFINITIV - El Futuro Del Trading, La Tecnología En 2024 ES

trends.name: Value-Based and Purpose-Driven Purchasing Coexisting
trends.description: Consumer purchasing behavior simultaneously driven by economic value-seeking and purpose/values alignment — with neither dominating, creating unpredictable patterns that defy conventional segmentation.

trends.name: Restaurant as Primary Third Place
trends.description: Restaurants cementing their role as society's primary third place — with dining out becoming a lifestyle mainstay and leisure centerpiece as home and work environments lose their social distinctiveness.

trends.name: AI Search Disruption as Existential Journalism Challenge
trends.description: AI-powered search fundamentally disrupting the referral model that has sustained online journalism — with generative AI answers reducing click-through to news sources and threatening publisher revenue structures.

trends.name: Creatorfication of News
trends.description: News production and distribution being reshaped by individual journalist-creators building direct audience relationships — with personality-driven journalism competing with and sometimes outperforming institutional brands.

trends.name: AI Sweeping Through Information Space
trends.description: Generative AI disrupting the information ecosystem simultaneously across content production, search, distribution, and trust — with implications for media sustainability, information reliability, and democratic discourse.

trends.name: Platform Referral Model Ending for Publishers
trends.description: Social media platform algorithm changes reducing outbound link sharing, cutting referral traffic to news publishers — ending the model where social platforms were a primary audience acquisition channel.

trends.name: Goldilocks Era Ending for Financial Markets
trends.description: The Goldilocks economic environment of 2023 (strong growth + falling inflation) not sustainable into 2024 — with higher-for-longer rates, slowing growth, and ESG regulatory expansion creating a more complex investment landscape.

trends.name: AI Widely Implemented Across Enterprise
trends.description: Enterprise AI moving from experimentation to wide implementation — with the majority of organizations reporting AI broadly deployed and driving operational outcomes rather than isolated in pilot programs.

trends.name: Generative AI Eclipsing All Other AI Categories
trends.description: Generative AI rapidly dominating organizational AI investment and attention — eclipsing traditional ML, RPA, and predictive analytics as the primary focus of enterprise AI strategy and spending.

trends.name: Slowing Dragons, Roaring Tigers in APAC
trends.description: APAC economic growth prospects shifting from China/Japan/Australia toward Southeast Asia and India — with Taiwan, Vietnam, Singapore, and India accelerating as China slows, reshaping the region's investment geography.

sources.report: S&P Global - APAC Sector Roundup Q1 2024

trends.name: New Risks, New Playbook for Credit Markets
trends.description: Return to higher real interest rates ending the cheap money era — requiring fundamentally different credit analysis, liquidity management, and issuer strategy as refinancing costs rise and economic activity slows.

trends.name: Four Global Structural Shifts Defining the Decade
trends.description: Santander identifying four simultaneous global structural shifts — in energy, geopolitics, technology use, and financing access — requiring investors to extend horizons beyond short-term rate cycles.

trends.name: Trump 47 Repricing Market Expectations
trends.description: Donald Trump's 2024 election victory causing markets to reprice US growth expectations higher — with investors weighing whether pro-growth policies can offset inflationary forces of his protectionist agenda.

trends.name: Industrial Metaverse Evolving from IIoT to Digital Twin
trends.description: Industrial technology evolving through successive layers — from Industrial IoT to digital twin to industrial metaverse — with each layer adding richer simulation, collaboration, and predictive capability for manufacturing operations.

trends.name: Entertainment as Brand Strategy
trends.description: Brands winning by becoming genuine entertainment entities — earning share of voice through creativity and cultural relevance rather than buying it through paid media, competing on entertainment value against legacy brand effects.

sources.report: SMALLWORLD - How Brands Win In The Entertainment Era_CAIG

trends.name: Generative AI Dominant Tech Story for Foreseeable Future
trends.description: Generative AI and LLMs established as the dominant technology narrative — not a passing trend but the foundational transformation affecting enterprise data, applications, cybersecurity, and all technical roles.

trends.name: LLMs Transforming All Technical Roles
trends.description: Large language models beginning to fundamentally transform the nature of technical roles — from data scientists to software engineers to security professionals — requiring new skills, workflows, and value propositions.

trends.name: Human Essence Separating from Technology
trends.description: Growing concern about preserving human values, relationships, and experience as technology penetrates deeper into daily life — with the question of what is irreducibly human becoming a central design and ethical challenge.

trends.name: Private Cloud Infrastructure Shift
trends.description: Enterprise IT shifting toward private cloud infrastructure as security, control, and cost management concerns outweigh the public cloud advantages — creating a more hybrid, sovereignty-aware infrastructure landscape.

trends.name: AI and Tech Talent Gap as Strategic Constraint
trends.description: Shortage of AI and technology talent becoming a primary strategic constraint on digital transformation — with hiring challenges in AI disciplines slowing deployment of AI strategies that organizations are strategically committed to.

trends.name: GenAI from Hype to Value — ROI Imperative
trends.description: Generative AI entering the value realization phase — with organizations moving from deployment and experimentation to demonstrating measurable business returns that justify the unprecedented infrastructure investment.

trends.name: From LLMs to Modular AI Systems
trends.description: Enterprise AI architecture evolving from monolithic large language model deployments toward modular, composable AI systems — enabling more targeted, cost-effective, and controllable AI applications across business functions.

trends.name: 2030 Sustainability Deadline as Forcing Function
trends.description: The 2030 IPCC halving deadline and SDG target date operating as genuine forcing functions for innovation — compressing the timeline for sustainable technology deployment and creating urgency that earlier climate targets lacked.

trends.name: Authentic Unfiltered Photography as Design Counterweight
trends.description: Photography in design trending toward real, unfiltered imagery as a deliberate counterweight to AI-generated visual content — with genuine human imperfection and specificity becoming premium design signals.

trends.name: Great Unconformity — Tectonic Cultural Aesthetic Shift
trends.description: A fundamental, tectonic shift in visual culture and aesthetics predicted for 2025 — driven by AI, climate change, and political instability converging to create demand for authenticity, warmth, and human imperfection in visual language.

trends.name: Old Soul Energy and Craft Revival in Visual Culture
trends.description: Visual culture trending toward collecting, curating, handcraft, and 'old money' aesthetics — a reaction to digital acceleration that finds value in patina, history, and the irreproducible qualities of physical objects.

trends.name: Social Fitness as Primary Sport Motivation
trends.description: Sport and fitness becoming primarily social activities — with run clubs, workout partnerships, and community-based fitness displacing individual performance metrics as the dominant motivation for physical activity.

trends.name: Consistency Over Heroics in Fitness Culture
trends.description: Fitness culture shifting from extreme challenges and peak performance to sustainable consistency — with rest, recovery, and long-term habit formation replacing the achievement-obsessed hustle culture of the 2010s.

trends.name: Motivation Science Replacing Raw Achievement in Fitness
trends.description: Sport and fitness culture shifting from pure achievement metrics toward understanding and optimizing the psychological drivers of motivation — with 'why' and 'how' becoming as important as performance data.

trends.name: Pay Equity as Strategic Business Advantage
trends.description: Workplace equity transitioning from compliance requirement to strategic business differentiator — with leading organizations proactively using pay equity analysis and demographic transparency as talent and brand assets.

trends.name: Employment Outcome Equity Beyond Pay
trends.description: Equity analysis expanding beyond pay to encompass all employment outcomes — promotions, performance reviews, retention, and career development — as the full dimensions of workplace fairness become visible and actionable.

trends.name: AI as Most Profound Change in Human History
trends.description: Leading AI researchers (including Stuart Russell) characterizing AI's emergence as the most profound change in human history — with both positive and negative outcomes possible depending on how humanity manages the transition.

sources.report: TALENT ALPHA_The Future of Work Report AI-ed 2024

trends.name: Cultural Edges with Scale and Longevity
trends.description: TBWA's Backslash methodology identifying deep cultural shifts with genuine staying power — as distinct from surface trends — tracking how major global forces like climate, AI, and identity are shifting trajectory.

trends.name: Climate Credit as Emerging Cultural Force
trends.description: A new cultural dynamic emerging around climate credibility and accountability — with individuals, brands, and institutions seeking and granting 'climate credit' based on demonstrated environmental action rather than stated intention.

trends.name: Long-Term Brand-Creator Collaborations Replacing Transactional Influencer Deals
trends.description: Influencer marketing evolving from one-off sponsored posts to sustained, long-term brand-creator partnerships — with relationship depth generating authenticity and consistency that short-term campaigns cannot achieve.

trends.name: Paid UGC as Professional Creator Category
trends.description: Paid user-generated content emerging as a distinct professional creator category — with brands commissioning UGC-style content that combines the authenticity of organic posts with the production control of branded content.

trends.name: Blockchain Infrastructure Maturing Post-Deleveraging
trends.description: Blockchain industry emerging from 2022-2023 credit crisis with stronger infrastructure — with composable DeFi, stablecoin proliferation, and scalable block space creating a more robust foundation for institutional adoption.

trends.name: Art as Strategic Business Differentiator
trends.description: Art and creative distinction emerging as a primary business strategy for cutting through a landscape of algorithmic uniformity — with genuine artistic investment creating the emotional connection that product features cannot.

sources.report: THE DRAWING ARM - Trend Report_Art will save the world_CAIG

trends.name: Era of Empowered Youth
trends.description: Children and young people entering 2025 as a newly empowered consumer and cultural force — with AI tools, digital platforms, and shifting family dynamics giving younger generations unprecedented agency and influence.

trends.name: Gaming Crossing into Real World — Agnostic Engagement
trends.description: Gaming mechanics, communities, and experiences extending into physical reality as an agnostic engagement platform — with game logic applied to education, social interaction, and brand experiences for younger consumers.

trends.name: Consumer Acceptance of New Circumstances
trends.description: 2025's most prevalent consumer trend: acceptance — consumers adapting to and making peace with new economic, technological, and social realities rather than resisting or mourning pre-disruption norms.

trends.name: Simplicity as Consumer Premium
trends.description: Consumers increasingly valuing simplicity — in products, interfaces, communication, and experiences — as a premium response to information overload, decision fatigue, and technological complexity.

trends.name: Digitally Powered Consumer Brand Wave
trends.description: A new generation of digitally native consumer brands building on functional wellness, sustainability, and personalization — with direct-to-consumer, content-led, and data-driven models outperforming legacy consumer goods incumbents.

trends.name: Future of Alcohol and Fun Food Emerging
trends.description: Alcohol category facing structural disruption as consumer preferences evolve — with non-alcoholic alternatives, functional beverages, and 'fun food' innovations redefining the social indulgence category for health-conscious consumers.

trends.name: Fashion Goes Grassroots — Bottom-Up Trend Creation
trends.description: Fashion trend creation shifting from runway-down to street-up — with TikTok, television, and everyday style displacing fashion weeks as the primary trend-setting force, empowering consumers to shape fashion direction.

trends.name: Resale as Investment in Enduring Value
trends.description: Fashion resale shifting from thrift-seeking to quality investment — with consumers paying more for pieces that will retain value, as 'what will last' replaces 'what is new' as the primary luxury purchase criterion.

trends.name: Employee Wellness as Comprehensive Life Infrastructure
trends.description: Employee wellness evolving beyond gym memberships to encompass fitness, nutrition, sleep, mindfulness, therapy, and healthy habits — with employers providing comprehensive wellbeing infrastructure rather than isolated benefits.

sources.report: THE WELLHUB - Trends Report_2024 in Review_CAIG

trends.name: Strength Training Dominance in Fitness Culture
trends.description: Strength and resistance training cementing its position as the dominant fitness category globally — displacing cardio-focused fitness as the primary wellbeing modality, with implications for employer wellness programs and consumer fitness brands.

sources.report: THE WELLHUB - Trends Report_2024 in Review_CAIG

trends.name: Brand Chemistry — Authenticity-Driven Platform Connection
trends.description: TikTok identifying 'Brand Chem' as the defining 2025 dynamic — brands succeeding through genuine chemistry with communities rather than transactional advertising, built through Brand Fusion, Identity Osmosis, and Creative Catalysts.

trends.name: Identity Osmosis on Social Platforms
trends.description: Consumer and brand identities permeating and influencing each other bidirectionally on TikTok — with the boundary between brand identity and community identity becoming fluid as co-creation replaces broadcast.

trends.name: Bravery That Breaks Through on Social
trends.description: Brand success on TikTok in 2024 defined by bravery — willingness to take creative risks, break from conventions, and show genuine personality — with safe, predictable content failing to generate meaningful engagement.

trends.name: Storytelling Unhinged — Unconventional Narrative Formats
trends.description: Most effective TikTok content abandoning conventional narrative structures in favor of unexpected, non-linear, emotionally direct storytelling that holds attention by being genuinely surprising.

trends.name: Intentional Dating Replacing Situationships
trends.description: Dating culture shifting toward clarity and intention — with singles explicitly seeking value-aligned, authentic connections and moving away from ambiguous 'situationships' as a deliberate response to emotional exhaustion.

trends.name: Boundaries as Workplace Non-Negotiable
trends.description: Employee boundaries in the workplace becoming a non-negotiable expectation — with right-to-disconnect policies, rejection of forced fun, and explicit boundary-setting transforming the employer-employee relationship.

trends.name: Human Energy Consciousness in the Workplace
trends.description: Growing awareness of human energy as a finite and manageable organizational resource — with employers and employees alike designing work around energy cycles, recovery, and sustainable performance rather than maximal output.

trends.name: China Consumer Turning Negative and Cautious
trends.description: Chinese consumer sentiment deteriorating as deflationary recession takes hold — with the growth optimism that characterized China's consumer market replaced by rational, cautious, and highly selective purchasing behavior.

trends.name: Brand and Pop Culture Becoming Indistinguishable
trends.description: The boundary between brand activity and organic pop culture moments dissolving — with brands both creating and being created by cultural movements in a feedback loop where commercial and cultural value are inseparable.

trends.name: Middle Market M&A as Dominant Deal Driver
trends.description: Middle market M&A demonstrating stability and pipeline strength as large-cap deals suffer from higher rates — with generational succession, consolidation cycles, and strategic divestiture creating robust deal flow.

trends.name: Industry Hybridization — Race for Data
trends.description: Industry boundaries blurring as data becomes the primary competitive asset — with cross-sector M&A accelerating as companies acquire data capabilities across traditional industry lines to compete in the platform economy.

trends.name: LoopLife — AI-Enabled Circular Economy Normalization
trends.description: AI making circular economy behaviors — resale, rental, repair, recycling — simpler and more accessible, normalizing looplife consumption patterns that previously required significant consumer effort and awareness.

trends.name: Sensescaping — Multisensory Experience Design
trends.description: Brands and spaces designing deliberately for multisensory experience — scent, sound, texture, taste — as a strategy for creating memorable encounters in an increasingly visual and digital-first world.

trends.name: Worthwise — Value Beyond Price
trends.description: Consumers demanding demonstrable worth — not just value for money but meaningful contribution to their lives, communities, and planet — raising the bar for what justifies consumer spending in a skeptical economy.

trends.name: Humanifesto — Brands Declaring Human Values
trends.description: Brands articulating explicit human values manifestos — taking clear positions on what human flourishing means and committing to it operationally — as a counter to AI-driven homogenization and purpose fatigue.

trends.name: Exchange Economy Replacing Expectation Economy
trends.description: The era of consumer expectations (brands delivering what consumers demand) giving way to an exchange economy — where brands and consumers collaborate to solve the world's most urgent problems together.

trends.name: Roaring 20s — Next Stage
trends.description: UBS framing the mid-2020s as the next stage of a 'Roaring 20s' decade — with rising equity markets, solid economic growth, and AI-driven technological breakthroughs defining the period despite pandemic, war, and societal challenges.

trends.name: Blended Finance Catalyzing Private Capital for Impact
trends.description: Blended finance emerging as a primary mechanism for mobilizing private capital toward sustainable development — using concessional capital to de-risk investments that commercial investors would otherwise avoid.

trends.name: First Trillion-Dollar AI Company Marking New Era
trends.description: 2023 seeing the first AI-focused company reach trillion-dollar valuation — marking a watershed moment that legitimizes AI as the defining investment and economic theme of the decade.

trends.name: Skills-Based Economy Replacing Credentials
trends.description: Labor market restructuring around demonstrated skills rather than formal credentials — with AI and automation driving demand for specific capabilities that traditional education systems cannot certify fast enough.

sources.report: UDEMY_ Global Learning & Skills Trends Report 2024

trends.name: Generative AI Skills as Urgency Learning Category
trends.description: Learning and development organizations identifying generative AI skills adaptation as an urgent organizational priority — with workers needing to rapidly develop AI collaboration competencies that formal education cannot deliver at speed.

sources.report: UDEMY_ Global Learning & Skills Trends Report 2024

trends.name: FemTech Entering Radical Opportunity Era
trends.description: Women's health technology at an inflection point — with AI-enabled diagnostics, wearable health monitoring, reproductive technology, and personalized medicine creating a decade of transformative opportunity in an historically underserved market.

trends.name: FemTech Market Maturation and Scope Expansion
trends.description: FemTech maturing beyond reproductive health into autoimmunity, AI companionship, and preventive care — with the term 'FemTech' expanding in definition as the market grows and diversifies into historically underserved women's health categories.

sources.report: ULTRA VIOLET_Femtech Future 30-The most influential trends shaping the market in 2024

trends.name: Creative Imagination as UN Futures Methodology
trends.description: UN Global Pulse deploying creative, artistic, and speculative imagination as a methodology for futures work — arguing that the 2030 Agenda requires imaginative capacity beyond conventional policy analysis to envision genuinely sustainable futures.

sources.report: UN GLOBAL PULSE_The most creative look to the future

trends.name: Development Landscape Undergoing Structural Change
trends.description: UNDP Strategy and Futures Team identifying the global development landscape as undergoing fundamental structural change — with emerging issues requiring new foresight approaches beyond traditional development frameworks.

trends.name: Youth-Centered Foresight as Rights-Based Practice
trends.description: Youth foresight emerging as a rights-based practice — with UNICEF arguing that young people have a right to participate in futures conversations, requiring accessible tools and methodologies that enable meaningful engagement rather than token inclusion.

trends.name: Security as Most Critical Enterprise Technology Priority
trends.description: Cybersecurity consistently ranking as the most critical technology area for enterprise decision-makers — with threat sophistication, regulatory pressure, and AI-enabled attack vectors making security the unavoidable foundation of all technology strategy.

trends.name: Conversational AI Transforming Customer Experience Infrastructure
trends.description: Conversational AI, CCaaS, and intelligent automation converging to transform customer experience infrastructure — with enterprises redesigning customer interactions around AI-native architectures rather than retrofitting AI into existing CX stacks.

trends.name: Inflation Navigation Reshaping Youth Fashion Consumption
trends.description: Inflation forcing a fundamental recalibration of youth fashion consumption — with younger consumers developing sophisticated value navigation strategies that blend resale, rental, investment buying, and selective splurging as permanent adaptations to constrained economics.

trends.name: Empathetic AI as Emerging Cultural Code
trends.description: AI designed and experienced through an empathetic lens emerging as a distinct cultural code — with youth consumers evaluating AI not primarily on capability but on whether it feels understanding, supportive, and emotionally responsive.

trends.name: Made by Humans — Human Creativity as Premium Signal
trends.description: Human-made creativity emerging as an explicit premium differentiator in an AI-saturated creative economy — with 'Made by Humans' becoming a quality signal, provenance claim, and cultural value statement as AI-generated content normalizes.

trends.name: Analog Movement — Tactile and Physical Experience Revival
trends.description: A broad analog movement gaining cultural momentum — with tactile activities, physical media, and non-digital experiences attracting consumers seeking grounding, presence, and genuine sensory engagement as counterweight to digital acceleration.

trends.name: AIdentities — AI-Enabled Digital Identity Construction
trends.description: AI enabling new forms of digital identity construction — with very lifelike AI personas, digital avatars, and AI-mediated self-expression creating an identity economy where the boundary between human and artificial self-presentation blurs.

trends.name: Climate-Adaptive Lives as Design Imperative
trends.description: Designing for climate-adaptive living emerging as a core innovation imperative — with products, services, and communities being redesigned around the assumption of ongoing climate disruption rather than treating climate as an exceptional event.

trends.name: Personal and Cultural Relevance as Media Future
trends.description: The future of media built on personal and cultural relevance — with brands that establish authentic viewpoints within consumer identity and cultural communities generating trust, loyalty, and commercial outcomes that attention-based advertising cannot replicate.

trends.name: Generation Alpha Entering Consumer Consciousness
trends.description: Generation Alpha — the largest generation in history, born 2010-2024 — beginning to register as a distinct consumer force, with gaming, digital-native behaviors, and climate awareness shaping a generational profile meaningfully different from Gen Z.

trends.name: Return to Simplicity as Cultural Counter-Trend
trends.description: A deliberate return to simplicity emerging as a cultural counter-trend — with consumers, particularly younger ones, choosing less complex, less mediated, and less optimized lives in reaction to digital saturation and information overload.

trends.name: Polycrisis Intensifying — Climate and Conflict Converging
trends.description: WEF Global Risks Report framing the global risk landscape as a converging polycrisis — with climate change and geopolitical conflict amplifying each other's effects, creating compounding instability that single-domain responses cannot address.

trends.name: AI Governance Deficit as Systemic Risk
trends.description: The gap between AI capability and AI governance identified as a systemic global risk — with AI systems making consequential decisions faster than regulatory, institutional, and social frameworks can adapt, creating structural vulnerability.

trends.name: Cybersecurity as Collective Resilience Imperative
trends.description: Cybersecurity evolving from an individual organizational responsibility to a collective resilience challenge — requiring coordinated action across governments, industries, and civil society to build trustworthy digital infrastructure at the scale modern economies depend on.

trends.name: Digital Jobs as Global Economic Inclusion Infrastructure
trends.description: WEF/Capgemini identifying the rise of global digital jobs as a structural economic shift — with technology adoption, green transition, and remote work jointly creating opportunities for digitally skilled workers across geographies previously excluded from high-value employment.

sources.report: WEF_The Rise of Global Digital Jobs 2024

trends.name: Consumer Fragmentation into Distinct Value Archetypes
trends.description: WGSN identifying four distinct consumer archetypes for 2026 — Impartialists (facts over fiction), Autonomists (self-determined values), Gleamers (simpler life), and a fourth profile — representing a fragmentation of consumer identity into clearly differentiated value orientations that require targeted strategies.

trends.name: Responsible Metaverse Standards Codifying
trends.description: WEF Global Future Council on Metaverse codifying responsible metaverse standards — establishing a maturity model that defines what responsible technology integration looks like across emerging, developing, and mature organizational stages of metaverse adoption.

sources.report: WORLD ECONOMIC FORUM - Responsible Metaverse Maturity Model

trends.name: Acute Food Insecurity at Humanitarian Scale
trends.description: Global acute food insecurity operating at humanitarian scale — with 693+ million people lacking sufficient food consumption across 87 countries, 193 million experiencing acute hunger in 53 countries, and 828 million chronically undernourished, requiring systemic rather than episodic response.

sources.report: WORLD FOOD PROGRAMME_HungerMap-Global insights and key trends

trends.name: Creative Technology as Primary Brand Engagement Platform
trends.description: AI, spatial computing, and virtual worlds converging into a creative technology platform for brand engagement — with brands no longer choosing between digital channels but building integrated immersive experiences that meet virtual-native consumers on their own terms.

trends.name: Fans as Active Creators Reshaping Culture
trends.description: Fandom shifting from passive consumption to active cultural creation — with fans producing, remixing, and distributing content that shapes popular culture at scale, blurring the boundary between audience and author and creating a new model of culture formation.

trends.name: Democratization of Creative Tools Redefining Cultural Power
trends.description: Rapid democratization of creative tools — AI-enhanced video, editing software, distribution platforms — redistributing cultural power from professional gatekeepers to everyday creators, enabling fan-driven content to achieve mainstream reach and influence.

trends.name: Digital Franchise Emerging as New Cultural IP Model
trends.description: The digital franchise emerging as a new model for cultural intellectual property — with creator-driven content universes, fan communities, and platform-native cultural touchstones developing franchise-like properties that rival traditional entertainment IP in reach and loyalty.

trends.name: Human-Centric AI as Customer Experience Differentiator
trends.description: Human-centric AI — AI that enhances rather than replaces human connection in customer experience — emerging as the defining differentiator between CX leaders and laggards, with 70% of consumers already perceiving a clear gap forming between organizations that use AI well and those that don't.

scenarios.name: Foresight as Public Infrastructure
scenarios.description: By 2035, strategic foresight tools and outputs become open-source public goods accessible beyond institutional gatekeepers, fundamentally democratizing futures literacy.

scenarios.name: World of Anomalies
scenarios.description: A scenario where positive economic outlook coexists with persistent structural anomalies — high debt, geopolitical fragmentation, tech disruption — requiring investors to navigate without historical precedent.

scenarios.name: The New Health CEO
scenarios.description: A future where consumers function as their own health executives — directing care pathways, evaluating providers, and using AI tools to make informed medical decisions with unprecedented autonomy.

scenarios.name: Across-the-Board Tariff Scenario
scenarios.description: Goldman Sachs scenario analysis of broad US tariffs — more inflationary, more dollar-positive, and more risk-off than baseline, representing the key tail risk for 2025 market outcomes.

scenarios.name: Beautiful Game Scenario
scenarios.description: Helaba's 10% probability positive scenario where global growth surprises upward, inflation normalizes, and financial conditions ease — setting the stage for broad risk asset appreciation.

scenarios.name: Three Tensions for 2023 Home Life
scenarios.description: IKEA scenario framework identifying three defining tensions shaping domestic life in 2023: doing more vs. doing less, togetherness vs. privacy, and living well vs. living within means.

scenarios.name: Ozempic-Driven Consumption Scenario
scenarios.description: A future consumer landscape reshaped by GLP-1 drug adoption — where reduced appetite, lower food consumption, and new notions of satiety create structural shifts in food, beverage, fitness, and fashion markets.

scenarios.name: China Property Crisis Deepening Scenario
scenarios.description: S&P's downside scenario where China's property crisis deepens significantly — with cascading effects on domestic consumption, banking system stability, and regional trade that could substantially slow Asian economic growth.

sources.report: S&P Global - APAC Sector Roundup Q1 2024

scenarios.name: AI Creatures with Human Emotions — 2071
scenarios.description: Speculative fiction scenario set in 2071 where AI entities manage human society with cancelled human interaction functions — exploring the philosophical and emotional implications of AI systems inheriting human emotional architecture while optimizing for systemic stability.

scenarios.name: Positive and Negative AI Futures Both Available
scenarios.description: Talent Alpha/Intelligent Leadership Hub scenario framework showing that both positive and negative AI transition outcomes are genuinely available — with human choices about governance, design, and deployment determining which materializes.

sources.report: TALENT ALPHA_The Future of Work Report AI-ed 2024

scenarios.name: FemTech 2035 — AI-Enabled Women's Health Revolution
scenarios.description: Ultra Violet's 10-year scenario where AI diagnostics, personalized medicine, and reproductive technology converge to fundamentally transform women's health outcomes — if ethically governed and equitably distributed.

scenarios.name: AI in Charge — 2026 Risk Scenario
scenarios.description: WEF Global Risks Report scenario where AI systems assume consequential decision-making authority across critical domains by 2026 — examining the governance failures, economic disruptions, and social instabilities that could result from premature AI delegation.
